rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHRISTINE YOUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Close
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. (1) Letters have not been issued. (2) A written and verified report of the estate bank account has not been filed. (3) Petition for final distribution and final report has not been filed. If such a petition cannot be filed at this time, a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. Code sec. 12200 et seq. two weeks before the hearing. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VICTOR CADIZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/18/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. [LRSF 14.14 A.] The amendment was not e-filed (available for viewing) until 7/14/25, which was also not at least 15 days before this hearing. (2) The seller's closing statement attached as Exhibit B to the amendment does not appear to be for the sale of the estate's asset. Please provide a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/4/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Joseph Cadiz was never appointed as the executor of the will, as alleged in paragraph 4 of the amendment but correctly noted in paragraph 12.f. of the amendment. The sixth amended accounting(s) altogether covered the period 3/4/16 - 5/23/24, not 4/9/21 through 5/31/23)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF QUEENIE ANDERSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/25/25. Examiner's Notes emailed to attorney with the following issues: 1) Petitioner alleges that Decedent died on 9/9/07, but the order for probate indicates she died on 9/23/07. 2) Please allege whether any action was taken on the rejected claim described in paragraph 15. 3) The personal representatives or, if none, the successors in interest of Joanne Anderson and Donald Anderson, have not been identified to determine who is entitled to distribution and/or notice. Please be sure to specify whether personal representatives have been appointed. 4) In par. 43, Petitioner requests a reserve for, among other things, to pay any additional attorneys' fees. No additional attorney fees may be paid without further petition. 5) Notice of Hearing has not been filed. 6) The declaration re: xo fees referenced in par. 42 has not been filed. 7) Ending balance of cash must be verified [Local Rule 14.34D]. Please also make sure to file a verified declaration pursuant to Local Rule 14.59A certifying that the original document(s) will be available for inspection in the same way as documents are maintained under CRC 2.257. 8) Proposed order has not been filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LOVIE MAE PHILLIPS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/6/25 at Counsel's request to address the following issues: (1) Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. (2) When the petition seeks a non-pro rata distribution, it must show the computation on which the proposed distribution is based. Consents of interested beneficiaries must be filed. [LRSF 14.35 I.6.a.ii.] Courtesy copies of the supplement to the petition or any other papers filed to address the issues must be received by the assigned examiner by 9/22/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CLARENCE E JACKSON JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/29/25. The following threshold issues must be addressed prior to a full review of the petition: 1) Many required allegations in a final distribution petition are missing. An "Amended Petition for Final Distribution" must be filed. 2) In addition to the missing allegations, the following are also noted, upon a cursory review: all calculations must be provided for statutory fees, detailed itemizations must be provided for requested costs, the specific assets on hand as of a date certain must be provided, specific distributions must be proposed after fees/costs with calculations, support to show that the estate should be shared 50/50 by spouse and child must be provided, and where cash assets exceed $250,000, proof that all assets are fully FDIC insured must be filed. 3) Notice of Hearing has not been filed on the required Judicial Council Form (DE-120). Please be sure to give notice on the filing of the amended petition that will be filed. 4) Non-cash assets of the estate must be listed in Attachment 2 of the Inventory and Appraisal and appraised by the appointed probate referee listed in the order appointing administrator, which was filed on 5/9/24. The revised Inventory and Appraisal must be captioned as "Partial No. 1" and "Corrected". In addition, a separate "Final" Inventory and Appraisal filed (if no additional assets exist, it should indicate $0). 5) Proposed order should be e-filed. All documents to be filed by 9/15/25. * Tentative Ruling posted on 7/10/25.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HAROLD J. MILLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/22/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number e-filed on 7/9/25 was just a cover sheet. (3) Not all persons mentioned in the will were listed in item 8 of the petition and were given notice. The will names two remote, contingent beneficiaries. (4) Item 1 of the petition did not disclose the name of the newspaper. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 9/8/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
